sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Osasuna have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with a thrilling 5-3 win against CD Ebro but struggling in La Liga with only 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses. Levante UD, on the other hand, are in poor form, losing 4 of their last 5 matches across competitions, including a narrow 1-0 win in the Copa del Rey. This gives Osasuna a slight edge going into the match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these teams, Osasuna have dominated with a 3-1 victory in March 2022 and a 0-0 draw in December 2021. Osasuna's ability to score early and maintain pressure has been evident, making them the favorites in historical context.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Osasuna, sitting 17th with 12 points, have struggled offensively, scoring just 12 goals (19th in La Liga), but their defense is mid-table, conceding 18 goals. Levante UD, bottom of the league with 9 points, have conceded 26 goals while scoring 16, showing defensive vulnerabilities that Osasuna could exploit.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Osasuna, Ante Budimir (4 goals) and Raúl García de Haro (3 goals in recent form) will be crucial in attack. Levante's Etta Eyong (6 goals, 3 assists) poses a threat, but their overall form and missing defenders might hinder their performance.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Outlook
sections.text: Osasuna are likely to leverage their strength in aerial duels (3rd in La Liga) and target Levante's leaky defense with crosses and set pieces. Levante, often first to concede in recent matches, may struggle to contain Osasuna’s attacking intent at Estadio El Sadar.

sections.subtitle: Prediction
sections.text: Given Osasuna's home advantage, recent head-to-head dominance, and Levante's poor form and defensive issues, a home win seems probable. Sofascore's 51% probability for an Osasuna victory aligns with the data, though a tight contest is expected.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half dominance by Osasuna
halftimeSections.text: Osasuna have taken a commanding lead in the first half with a 2-0 scoreline at Estadio El Sadar. Their clinical finishing was evident with goals from Victor Muñoz and Rubén García, showcasing their ability to capitalize on opportunities. Despite having less possession compared to Levante UD, Osasuna made the most of their entries into the final third and penalty area. Their aerial dominance and duel-winning ability further highlight their physical edge in this matchup. Rubén García, in particular, stood out with a goal and an assist, proving to be a key figure in their attack.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Levante UD's struggle to convert
halftimeSections.text: Levante UD, on the other hand, have struggled to turn their dominance in possession into meaningful results. Despite having more of the ball and numerous entries into the final third, they failed to create significant chances. Their shots on target were on par with Osasuna, but the lack of quality in the final delivery has left them trailing. Defensively, they’ve been vulnerable, particularly against set pieces and direct play, as seen in the goals conceded. Levante will need to sharpen their attacking output if they are to mount a comeback in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Osasuna start with strong attacking intent, maintaining pressure in the early stages. Their persistence paid off with an early goal, keeping the momentum in their favor for much of the opening period. Levante UD, however, fought back with sustained attacking spells around the midway point, pushing hard to create opportunities. Despite their efforts, Osasuna regained control later in the half, securing their second goal. Towards the end of the half, Levante showed signs of resurgence with renewed attacking vigor, but they couldn’t break through Osasuna’s defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Osasuna, Rubén García has been the standout performer, contributing both a goal and an assist in the first half. His creativity and composure in the final third will be crucial for maintaining their lead. Victor Muñoz, with his well-taken header, also poses a constant threat. On Levante’s side, Iván Romero has shown glimpses of potential with his involvement in attack, and he could be the spark they need. Their midfield will rely on players like Unai Vencedor to dictate play and create chances in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Osasuna are likely to adopt a more compact defensive shape in the second half to protect their lead while looking to hit Levante on the counter. Their focus will be on maintaining structure and exploiting spaces left by a potentially desperate Levante side. Levante UD, meanwhile, will almost certainly push for an early goal to get back into the game, likely committing more players forward. This could expose them to counterattacks, but they have little choice but to take risks. Adjustments in their attacking setup will be key to breaking down Osasuna’s resolute defense.
